steps.title: Use the two given values
steps.content: The function has the form $f(x)=ax+b$ and passes through $(1,-64)$ and $(2,0)$. The slope is $$a=\frac{0-(-64)}{2-1}=64.$$

steps.title: Solve for b and answer
steps.content: Substitute $(2,0)$ into $f(x)=64x+b$: $$0=64(2)+b,$$ so $b=-128$. Therefore $$a-b=64-(-128)=192.$$ D is correct. This also checks the two original values: $64(1)-128=-64$ and $64(2)-128=0$.
